campbell.title: Campbell City Council — March 24, 2026
campbell.summary: Campbell City Council met on March 24, 2026 to honor K9 Lucas, the police department's first working dog who recently passed away. The council also moved forward on several items including an updated beekeeping ordinance, a study about creating homeless services in the West Valley region, and a public hearing on state transit-oriented development rules that had three council members step aside due to property conflicts.
keyTopics: K9 Lucas memorial and recognition
Updated beekeeping ordinance approved
West Valley regional homeless services task force recommended
SB 79 transit-oriented development hearing (3 council members recused)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 31, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a special meeting to review and approve work plans for five different city commissions that handle everything from parks and libraries to traffic safety and planning. Each commission presented their priorities and goals for the next fiscal year. The council also met privately to discuss a legal case the city may pursue.
keyTopics: Parks and Recreation Commission plans for 2026-27
Heritage Preservation Commission priorities
Library and Public Art Commission initiatives
Traffic Safety Commission work plan
Planning Commission priorities

los-altos.title: Los Altos City Council — March 24, 2026
los-altos.summary: The Los Altos City Council met to handle routine administrative business including reviewing meeting minutes and financial reports. The council approved several resolutions related to weed control, affordable housing policies, and authorized the raising of Pride and Juneteenth flags at city facilities. They also received a presentation from the county transit authority and discussed plans for implementing new housing policies.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program completion and final report
Affordable housing unit transfer policy changes
Progress Pride and Juneteenth flag raisings approved
County transit authority (VTA) presentation
Housing Element implementation timeline

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — March 17, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on March 17, 2026 to approve meeting minutes, review the city's finances from January, and move forward with several projects including a new residential development called Solana and updates to community health services. The council also discussed potential changes to how they format and keep official meeting records.
keyTopics: Solana Project development approved - new residential tract moving forward
January 2026 financial report reviewed - city budgets and investments on track
Community Health and Senior Services work plan set for 2026
General Plan and Housing Element progress report presented
Changes to official meeting minute procedures and formats

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 1, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a planning meeting to organize their upcoming agendas for April 7th and April 14th, 2026. They reviewed what items to add to these meeting schedules, decided which topics needed specific time slots, and checked whether any agenda items required special notice to the public.
keyTopics: April 7, 2026 Final Agenda - adding items and setting time slots
April 14, 2026 Draft Agenda - organizing discussion topics
Public record review from March 19-26, 2026
Notice requirements for agenda items and documents

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 30, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on March 30, 2026 where they approved previous meeting minutes and opened the floor for public comments and questions. The council also reviewed committee reports and discussed upcoming meetings for various city boards and commissions.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes
Public comment period
Committee and staff reports
Scheduling of board and commission meetings
Recreation Division contact for agenda questions (650-903-6331)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Housing and Human Services Commission met to review applications for federal grant money that helps pay for housing and community development projects in the city. The commission discussed which local organizations should receive funding from the Community Development Block Grant program to support housing and human services initiatives.
keyTopics: Federal grant applications for housing and community projects
Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding decisions
Review of February meeting minutes
Human services funding priorities

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed ways to encourage shop owners to get proper licenses, partnered with shopping centers to reach more businesses, and planned summer events and incentives to help retail stores and startups thrive.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program to help unlicensed shops get compliant
Outreach efforts targeting shopping centers and malls
Summer 'Shop Local' campaign to boost local retail
City sponsorships and partnerships with private businesses
Financial incentives for small businesses and startups

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 2, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara's Historical and Landmarks Commission held a hearing on April 2, 2026 to discuss matters related to the city's historic buildings and sites. Residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom to hear about and comment on the issues being reviewed.
keyTopics: Historical and Landmarks Commission hearing
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom)
Public comment submission deadline (12 p.m. day of meeting)
Discussion of historic buildings and landmarks in Santa Clara

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — March 17, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council held a routine meeting to handle administrative tasks and approve a few items. The council reviewed meeting schedules, approved previous meeting minutes, and authorized Mayor Montano to attend the WELL 2026 Annual conference.
keyTopics: Approved meeting calendar and minutes from previous sessions
Mayor Montano authorized to travel to WELL 2026 Annual conference
Confirmed appointment of school district representative to Economic committee
Reviewed upcoming agenda items and council member requests

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to honor Women's History Month with a proclamation and approved two agreements: one allowing the American Red Cross to use city facilities during disasters, and another to purchase email security software (Mimecast) for three years at about $93,400 annually.
keyTopics: Women's History Month proclamation
Red Cross disaster relief facility agreement
Email security software purchase ($93,383/year for 3 years)
Late agenda items added to meeting packet
